operations.summary: List GitHub Projects using a fixed GraphQL query.

graphql.document: query ListProjects($owner: String!, $repo: String!, $first: Int!, $after: String) { repository(owner: $owner, name: $repo) { owner { __typename login ... on Organization { projectsV2(first: $first, after: $after) { nodes { id number title url closed updatedAt } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } } } ... on User { projectsV2(first: $first, after: $after) { nodes { id number title url closed updatedAt } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } } } } } }

operations.summary: List GitHub Project v2 items using a fixed GraphQL query.

graphql.document: query ListProjectItems($projectId: ID!, $first: Int!, $after: String) { node(id: $projectId) { ... on ProjectV2 { id number title items(first: $first, after: $after) { nodes { id type createdAt updatedAt content { __typename ... on Issue { id number title url state } ... on PullRequest { id number title url state } ... on DraftIssue { id title } } } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } } } } }

operations.summary: List repository discussions using a fixed GraphQL query.

graphql.document: query ListDiscussions($owner: String!, $repo: String!, $first: Int!, $after: String) { repository(owner: $owner, name: $repo) { discussions(first: $first, after: $after, orderBy: {field: UPDATED_AT, direction: DESC}) { nodes { id number title url isAnswered answerChosenAt createdAt updatedAt author { login } category { id name slug } } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } } } }

operations.summary: View one repository discussion using a fixed GraphQL query.

graphql.document: query ViewDiscussion($owner: String!, $repo: String!, $number: Int!) { repository(owner: $owner, name: $repo) { discussion(number: $number) { id number title body url isAnswered answerChosenAt createdAt updatedAt author { login } category { id name slug } comments(first: 20) { totalCount nodes { id body url isAnswer createdAt updatedAt author { login } } } } } }

operations.summary: Delete an issue using a fixed GraphQL mutation.
operations.approval: destructive reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute

graphql.document: mutation DeleteIssue($issueId: ID!) { deleteIssue(input: { issueId: $issueId }) { clientMutationId } }

operations.summary: Download release assets with a bounded file-transfer policy.
operations.approval: filesystem writes require an explicit destination policy

operations.summary: Clone a repository through a restricted local Git workflow.
operations.approval: local filesystem and git mutations require explicit approval

operations.summary: Create or update a repository Actions secret (policy-modeled, not executable in this issue).
operations.approval: plan, preview, approval, execute plus typed confirmation
